response.description: A DropdownMenu is a UI component that displays a list of choices or actions in a compact, collapsible menu. It provides an efficient way to present multiple options without cluttering the interface, appearing contextually or on user interaction. The component supports various types of menu items, including selectable options, action triggers, and submenus, and handles keyboard navigation, focus management, and accessibility out-of-the-box. It is ideal for navigation menus, action lists associated with an element, user profile options, or filtering/sorting controls.

minimal.description: A simple dropdown menu with a trigger button and two basic menu items.
minimal.title: Basic Dropdown Menu

common.code: import * as DropdownMenu from "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu"; import { LogOut, Settings, User } from "lucide-react"; export function UserActionsDropdown() { return ( console.log("Profile")}> Profile console.log("Settings")}> Settings console.log("Log out")}> Log out ); }
common.title: Dropdown Menu with Actions and Icons
common.description: An example demonstrating a dropdown menu with actions, each accompanied by an icon and a separator for grouping related items.

common.title: Dropdown Menu with Selectable Theme Options
common.description: A dropdown menu illustrating how to implement selectable options, such as theme settings, using radio group functionality and indicators for the currently selected item.
common.code: import * as DropdownMenu from "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu"; import { Check, Monitor, Moon, Sun } from "lucide-react"; import React from "react"; export function ThemeSelectorDropdown() { const [theme, setTheme] = React.useState("system"); return ( Light Dark System ); }

guidance.whenToUse: Use when you need to offer a list of actions or options that don't need to be constantly visible, saving screen space. It's suitable for secondary actions, navigation items, or selection controls within a limited area, especially when space is at a premium or options are numerous.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Avoid using for primary navigation elements that require constant visibility. If there are only one or two options, consider a Button or ToggleGroup instead. Do not use for critical, immediate actions that need to be clearly visible and accessible without an extra click.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure proper keyboard navigation with arrow keys to move between items, Enter to select an item, and Escape to close the menu. Implement ARIA roles like `menu`, `menuitem`, `menubutton`, `menuitemradio`, or `menuitemcheckbox` for semantic meaning depending on the item type. Manage focus appropriately upon opening (focusing on the first item) and closing (returning focus to the trigger). Ensure sufficient contrast for text and icons within the menu for users with low vision.
